name: amplitude-automation
description: Automate Amplitude tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): events, user activity, cohorts, user identification. Always search tools first for current schemas.
capabilities: Send events to Amplitude for tracking user actions
Retrieve user activity and event history from Amplitude
Find and identify users by various identifiers
Manage cohorts: list, view details, update membership
Browse event categories to discover available event types
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 87 static findings are false positives. The skill is documentation for Amplitude API operations via Rube MCP. Backtick characters in markdown code blocks were misidentified as shell execution. URLs and API operations are legitimate product analytics functionality, not security risks.
valueStatement: This skill enables Claude to automate Amplitude product analytics workflows including event tracking, user activity lookup, cohort management, and user identification through Rube MCP.
limitations: Requires Rube MCP server with active Amplitude connection
User ID resolution requires calling FIND_USER before activity lookup
Event timestamps must be in milliseconds (13-digit epoch)
Cohort membership updates are asynchronous and require status polling

useCases.targetUser: Product managers and developers building analytics into apps
useCases.title: Track User Events
useCases.description: Automatically send tracking events to Amplitude when users perform key actions in your application.

useCases.title: Analyze User Behavior
useCases.description: Look up user activity history to understand how users interact with your product.

useCases.title: Manage User Segments
useCases.description: Create and update cohort membership to segment users for targeted analysis or campaigns.

promptTemplates.title: Send Single Event
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the amplitude-automation skill to send a 'page_view' event to Amplitude with user_id 'user123'.

promptTemplates.title: Find User Activity
promptTemplates.prompt: Find user 'user456' in Amplitude, then get their recent activity.

promptTemplates.title: Update Cohort
promptTemplates.prompt: List the available cohorts, then add user 'user789' to the 'premium_users' cohort.

promptTemplates.title: Identify User Properties
promptTemplates.prompt: Set user properties for user 'user101' with plan='enterprise' and company='Acme Corp' using the IDENTIFY operation.

bestPractices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before use
Verify Amplitude connection status is ACTIVE before running workflows
Use batch events when sending multiple events to reduce API calls
Handle async operations like cohort updates with status polling
antiPatterns: Do not call GET_USER_ACTIVITY directly with application user IDs - must resolve to Amplitude internal ID first
Do not use seconds for timestamps - must use milliseconds (13-digit epoch)
Do not assume user property changes are immediate - they are eventually consistent

faq.question: What is Rube MCP?
faq.answer: Rube MCP is a Model Context Protocol server by Composio that provides integration with various tools including Amplitude analytics.

faq.question: Do I need an Amplitude API key?
faq.answer: No API key is required. The skill uses OAuth authentication through the Rube MCP connection flow.

faq.question: Why do I need to find the user first?
faq.answer: Amplitude uses internal user IDs separate from your application user IDs. FIND_USER resolves your user_id to Amplitude's internal ID required for activity queries.

faq.question: Why are my events not showing immediately?
faq.answer: Event ingestion is asynchronous. The API returns success but data takes a few minutes to become queryable in Amplitude dashboards.

faq.question: Can I update cohort membership in real-time?
faq.answer: Cohort membership updates are asynchronous. You must poll CHECK_COHORT_STATUS until the operation completes.

faq.question: What user property operations are available?
faq.answer: Amplitude IDENTIFY supports: $set (overwrite), $setOnce (set if not exists), $add (increment), $append (add to list), and $unset (remove).
